description: Fix RTL layout issues, topbar actions always on right, sidebar icons on right in RTL, complete i18n coverage for all hardcoded text in admin and portal, and fix language persistence from sidebar selector
steps: 1. Fix TheTopbar.vue RTL: The topbar-right uses margin-left:auto which should work in LTR. In RTL, add [dir=rtl] .topbar-right { margin-left: 0; margin-right: auto; } BUT we actually want topbar actions to ALWAYS stay on the right physical side regardless of RTL. So use position:fixed or keep margin-inline-start:auto pattern. Since flexbox direction is reversed in RTL, the margin-left:auto pushes items to the left (wrong). Fix: change to margin-inline-start: auto which respects logical direction, OR force direction:ltr on .topbar-right with explicit RTL override. Best approach: keep .topbar-right pinned to physical right with margin-left:auto AND add [dir=rtl] .topbar-right { margin-left: auto; margin-right: 0; } to override any RTL flex reversal. Actually the simplest fix is [dir=rtl] .topbar { direction: ltr; } on just the topbar container OR add explicit position styles. Recommended: use margin-inline-start: auto on .topbar-right (replaces margin-left:auto), and add [dir=rtl] .topbar-right { direction: ltr; } to prevent flexbox child reordering. The topbar-left content should still be RTL for text.

2. Fix TheSidebar.vue RTL nav-item: Currently has flex-direction: row-reverse for [dir=rtl] .nav-item which puts icon on right. User wants icons on RIGHT side in RTL (which IS the natural/start position for RTL). The current code already does flex-direction: row-reverse which puts icon last (right side). VERIFY this is correct: in LTR, nav-item is [icon] [label]. In RTL with row-reverse, it becomes [label] [icon] - icon is on the right side. This IS what user wants. If there's a bug, it may be that the sidebar border should be on left instead of right in RTL - already handled with :global([dir=rtl]) .sidebar { border-right: none; border-left: ... }. Ensure .badge also moves correctly.

3. Audit ALL .vue files for hardcoded English strings. Files to check: PaymentsView.vue has 'Record Payment', 'Approve', 'Reject', 'User', 'Amount', 'Method', 'Status', 'Intent', 'Date', 'Actions', 'Payment Methods', 'Add Method', 'No payment methods configured.', column labels, drawer titles, form labels. Add i18n keys for ALL of these.

4. In PaymentsView.vue: replace all hardcoded strings with t() calls. Add useI18n import. Add computed columns with translated labels. Translate drawer titles, form labels, button text, toast messages.

5. Check other views: TicketsView.vue, PlansView.vue, ResellersView.vue, CustomerDetailView.vue, LoginView.vue, SetupView.vue, TemplatesView.vue, NotificationsView.vue - audit each for hardcoded English and add t() calls + i18n keys.
6. In admin i18n.ts: add all new keys for all 3 locales (en, fa, zh) covering payments, tickets, plans, login, setup, templates, notifications views.
7. In portal i18n.ts: ensure SinglePageView.vue has NO hardcoded English - currently looks fully translated. Add 'Download Apps' section keys for the new app links section.

8. Fix sidebar language persistence: In TheSidebar.vue, handleChangeLang emits 'change-lang'. In AppShell.vue, handleChangeLang calls setLocale(locale as Locale). This SHOULD work because setLocale updates the ref which triggers the watch in useI18n.ts that calls localStorage.setItem. The issue might be that on page load, SettingsView.vue loadPanelSettings() reads the saved language from the SERVER and overrides localStorage. Fix: In loadPanelSettings(), only set the locale from server if no localStorage value exists (or if the localStorage and server value differ, prefer localStorage as the 'most recent' user choice). Alternatively, when sidebar changes lang, also save it to server via the same panel-settings PATCH endpoint.

9. Ensure all RTL CSS is consistent: portal header actions should ALWAYS stay on right (physical). PortalShell.vue .portal-header__actions uses margin-left:auto. Add [dir=rtl] .portal-header__actions { margin-left: auto; } to ensure it stays pinned right in RTL (flexbox in RTL with margin-left:auto should still work, but test and add explicit override if needed). Portal header should NOT flip.
acceptance_criteria: All user-facing text in ALL .vue files uses t() function calls with no hardcoded English strings (except brand names like 'KorisPanel')
Topbar actions (search, bell) always stay on the physical right side regardless of locale/RTL setting
In RTL mode, sidebar nav icons are on the right side (start position for RTL)
Language change from sidebar footer persists on refresh without being overridden
All 3 locales (en, fa, zh) have translations for every key used in templates
vue-tsc --noEmit passes for both admin and portal
verification: grep -r '"[A-Z][a-z]' panel/web/admin/src/views/ panel/web/portal/src/views/ --include='*.vue' | grep -v 't(' | grep -v 'import' | grep -v '//' | grep -v 'KorisPanel' - should find minimal or zero hardcoded strings
findings: All builds pass (vue-tsc --noEmit + vite build) for both admin and portal. RTL fix uses direction:ltr on topbar-right to prevent flexbox reordering in RTL while keeping topbar-left text RTL-aligned. Language persistence fixed by checking localStorage before applying server value. 196 new i18n keys added per locale. Sidebar titles and SettingsView export labels also translated. CustomerDetailView form labels use sed-based replacement which works correctly.
